element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Arm grab follow-ups on specific enemy types (Williams, Roper, Steve, Jackson, Chin brothers)
Progressive special attacks based on accumulated power; full gauge grants temporary attack strength boost
Williams and Roper enemies occasionally armed; player can disarm and use weapons (Baker's dao blades are exception—unobtainable)
Recurring sub-boss with Billy Lee moveset including hurricane kick
Kick-specialist martial artist; reappears as sub-boss in later stages
Former heavyweight boxer; reappears as sub-boss in later stages
Twin masters with leg-grab counter; Ron Foo (kicks) vs Ron Pyo (punches) require different responses
Stomach-blocking mechanic introduces unusual defense pattern
Giant gatekeeper; 'Death Leg' middle kick feared by comrades
Final boss with flashy martial arts style; culmination of all mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Dragon Power gauge full—temporary invulnerability window
typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounter (Steve, Jackson, Chin brothers, McGuire, Carlem, Duke)
Sub-boss recurrence (Steve, Jackson reappear after first encounter)
Jeff recurring sub-boss in early stages
Multi-enemy waves in confined casino/airport stages
Baker enemies with unobtainable weapon (twin dao blades)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Standard beat-em-up rhythm: enemy wave clearing → sub-boss encounter → boss fight. Relief punctuated by stage transitions. Escalating challenge through boss roster and reoccurring sub-boss patterns.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Mission 1-2 (Casino, Airport): Introduction of core mechanics (guard, arm grab, Dragon Power). Steve and Jackson bosses establish martial arts and boxing archetypes. Jeff sub-boss introduces special move usage.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mission 3-4 (Dojo, Moving truck): Chin brothers introduce dual-boss pattern with leg-grab counter mechanic. Mid-level challenge spike from previous stages.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mission 5-7 (Slum, Forest, Hideout): McGuire introduces unconventional defense (stomach block), Carlem features dangerous special kick, Duke final boss combines all learned patterns. Escalating enemy density and damage output.
notable_spikes: Mission 2 (Jackson)—boxing champion introduces heavy melee archetype
Mission 3 (Chin brothers)—first dual-boss; leg-grab counter requires tactical shift
Mission 5 (McGuire)—stomach-blocking mechanic breaks standard punch/kick rhythm

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear stage progression with contained arenas
notable_level_patterns.description: Seven sequential stages (casino → airport → dojo → truck → slum → forest → hideout) create geographical variety. Each stage presents enemy waves before boss encounter. No branching paths documented.
Steve (Mission 1 boss) and Jackson (Mission 2 boss) reappear as sub-bosses in subsequent stages, reinforcing learned patterns and introducing context for character differentiation.
Baker enemy type (always armed with dao blades) cannot be disarmed, creating exception to weapon pickup logic. Chin brothers grab and twist kicks, requiring different defensive approach.
Billy (agility/flexibility focus) vs Jimmy (strength focus) distinction mirrors stage variety: casinos/airports (agile navigation) to forest/hideout (strength-based combat).
